D7 Print L5 Evaluate Reference

Stochastic vs Clustered Dots

scattering many tiny dots resolves finer detail and dodges moiré — but all that extra dot edge drinks up more ink on press

Clustered-dot (AM) screening grows a few large dots; stochastic (FM) screening sprinkles many tiny ones. For the same tint, FM has far more total dot perimeter — so when ink spreads on press (dot gain), FM darkens more than AM. FM wins on detail and moiré-immunity; AM wins on press stability.

Set a flat tint and compare the two patterns side by side. Turn on press dot gain to grow every dot edge by the same amount — the stochastic patch jumps darker than the clustered one because it has so much more edge.
